title: Present — Educate Into Certainty
intro:
STEP 6 OF 8 · MODULE 6

Present — Educate Into Certainty

Mission: Convert findings into customer understanding. An educated customer authorizes; a confused one defers.

OBJECTIVE
EDUCATE
PACE
RE-ENGAGE 60-90s
PRIMARY KPI
PRESENT → AUTH %
FAILURE MODE
MONOLOGUE

sections.title: The Communication Map — Customer Confidence Across the Call
sections.html:
The Communication Map — Customer Confidence Across the Call

Figure 6.1 · Structured presentation moves customers up the confidence curve; monologue flatlines them

sections.title: Re-Engagement Loops — The 60-to-90-Second Rule
sections.html:
Re-Engagement Loops — The 60-to-90-Second Rule

The human attention span on a phone call is measured in tens of seconds. Long monologues lose the customer even when the content is correct. The discipline: every 60–90 seconds, insert a micro-question that re-engages the customer’s mind and confirms they are still tracking.

SCRIPTRe-engagement micro-questions

“Does that make sense so far?”
“Were you able to see that in the photo I sent?”
“Any questions on what we’ve covered so far?”
“Is this still the best number to reach you?”

PSYCHOLOGY DECODERWhy micro-questions multiply close rate.

Every “yes” the customer gives during the presentation is a small commitment that compounds toward the authorization “yes” at the end. By the time the advisor asks for the work, the customer has already said yes a half-dozen times. The final yes is just one more small step instead of a big leap.

sections.title: Worked Example — Gold-Standard 1st-Base ISO Safety Presentation
sections.html:
Worked Example — Gold-Standard 1st-Base ISO Safety Presentation

The following script (water-pump-leak example) is the current gold-standard 1st-base presentation. Every sentence maps to a deliberate move. Use this as the reference template for any 1st-base safety call.

SCRIPTBase-1 · ISO Safety Presentation — Water Pump Leak

1. Warm Opening & Time Check. “Hi [Customer], this is [Advisor] from [Shop]. Thanks for your patience — is now a good time to go over your vehicle?”

2. Advisor Co-Review (Credibility). “I personally reviewed your vehicle with our certified technician so I could give you a clear picture of what’s going on.”

3. Media Confirmation. “Were you able to view the photos/videos or the digital inspection report we sent over?”

4. Concern & Inspection. “You brought your vehicle in for an oil change. We’ve completed the initial inspection and I wanted to review what we found.”

5. Good News (positives). “The good news is your digital inspection report clearly documents everything with photos and notes so you can see exactly what we’re seeing, and we caught this early before it turned into a bigger issue.”

6. More Good News (discovery). “More good news — during our inspection we found that your water pump is leaking, which is separate from your original visit and something we can take care of now before it leads to further damage.”

7. Risk → Solution (with inherent service). “If left as-is, a leaking water pump can lead to engine overheating, coolant loss, and eventually major engine damage. The proper repair is to remove and replace the water pump, and complete a full cooling system service so everything is clean, sealed, and operating at the correct temperature.”

8. Total Investment. “The total investment for today’s work — including the cooling system service — is $1,695.46, parts and labor included.”

9. Coverage. “This work is covered by our 3-year / 36,000-mile nationwide warranty — parts and labor.”

10. Tee-Up & Callback. “While your vehicle is here, we’ll finish the full bumper-to-bumper digital inspection and I’ll reach out if anything else needs attention. Is this still the best number to reach you?”

Anatomy — What Each Step Is Doing

#StepWhat It’s Engineered To DoStep in OS
1Warm Opening & Time CheckRe-establishes rapport. The “is now a good time” gives the customer agency — reduces resistance.Step 1
2Co-Review (Credibility)“I personally reviewed” scores Competence + Trustworthy in one sentence.Step 2
3Media ConfirmationCustody check on the DVI. Locks in evidence before delivering the finding.Step 4
4Concern & InspectionPermission frame. Honors the original reason for the visit.Step 5 (Base 1)
5Good News (positives)First positive of Good-Good-Bad. Primes receptivity.Framework A
6More Good News (discovery)Second positive. Reframes a problem as a save. “Caught it early” = competence + caring.Framework A
7Risk → SolutionFull PRS. Risk is named explicitly — this is the urgency engine.Framework B
8Total InvestmentSingle number. No itemization (that’s Boss Battle 8 territory).Step 8
9CoverageWarranty named immediately after price. No pause between. This neutralizes sticker shock.Step 8
10Tee-Up & CallbackSets up Base 2 in advance. Asks for contact confirmation (commitment).Steps 5 & 8
PRO TIPNo pause between Investment and Coverage.

The single most common rookie mistake on a presentation call is pausing after stating the price — that pause is read by the customer as “wait for my reaction.” Coverage (warranty) must follow Investment in the same breath. Practice this until it’s muscle memory.

sections.title: Mastery Check
sections.html:
Mastery Check

LEVEL 6 · PRESENTATION MASTERY

  • Asked permission to speak before delivering findings.
  • Confirmed DVI was viewed (or summarized with permission).
  • Used Good-Good-Bad and full PRS on every finding.
  • Investment immediately followed by Coverage — no pause.
  • Re-engaged at least every 90 seconds with a micro-question.
  • Ended with a tee-up sentence for the next base.
COACH’S CORNERScore one presentation call per advisor per week against the 10-step anatomy.

Use the Worked Example table above as your scorecard. For each numbered step, mark Present / Partial / Missing. Coach the missing items one at a time. The most common gaps: skipping Risk (#7) and pausing between Investment and Coverage (#8/#9).

Present → Auth %
≥ 70%
% of presentations that end in authorization.
PRS Completeness
100%
% of findings with Problem, Risk, Solution stated in order.
DVI View Before Call
≥ 75%
Strong leading indicator of authorization.
